untranslatable_reason: Signal requires pair-trading spread analysis with Ornstein-Uhlenbeck process modeling, half-life calculation via regression (θ parameter), and spread z-score deviation detection. These require multi-asset correlation, statistical regression, and custom spread construction not available in single-asset indicator set. Allowed indicators cannot express: (1) spread construction between two stocks, (2) mean-reversion parameter estimation via linear regression, (3) half-life calculation, (4) z-score normalization of custom spread, (5) Ornstein-Uhlenbeck process validation.
translation_notes: This is a pairs trading signal requiring statistical arbitrage mechanics. The core logic involves: detecting when spread(P1-P2) deviates from its equilibrium mean by a calculable amount, then expecting reversion within a statistically-derived half-life period. Cannot be accurately represented using single-stock price-based indicators alone. Would require: (a) dual-asset feed, (b) cointegration/correlation analysis, (c) regression-based parameter fitting, (d) spread-specific z-score calculation.
